title: Field Briefing: Choose the Right Media Delivery Pattern
summary: Field Briefing: Choose the Right Media Delivery Pattern uses an original media delivery briefing scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html images alt, html svg, html audio, html video, html iframe without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real media delivery briefing artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

media delivery briefing phase 2.2: write or omit verbal content according to that purpose
media delivery briefing phase 2.3: provide intrinsic dimensions to reserve its aspect ratio
media delivery briefing phase 2.4: test the surrounding content with the image unavailable

steps.title: Read the media delivery briefing contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice follows the explanation and remains valid in a changed document? Work context: media delivery briefing.
steps.why: Field Briefing: Choose the Right Media Delivery Pattern requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 4 in the media delivery briefing.

content.text: media delivery briefing: Alternative text communicates an image’s purpose in context. Informative images need a concise equivalent; functional images name the action; complex images need a nearby extended explanation; and decorative images use an empty alt. Intrinsic width and height reserve the correct aspect ratio before the image loads. SVG can be loaded as an external image or embedded inline. Inline SVG exposes its internal structure to the document and can receive an accessible name; decorative SVG should be hidden from assistive technology. A viewBox defines scalable coordinates independently of rendered size. Native audio controls provide established playback behavior. Multiple source elements can offer compatible formats, fallback content supports browsers that cannot play them, and a full transcript gives equivalent access to the spoken information without requiring sound. A usable video exposes controls, dimensions, compatible sources, synchronized captions, and fallback content. Captions communicate dialogue and relevant sound; a transcript supports reading and search; and avoiding forced autoplay preserves user control. An iframe creates a nested browsing context. Its title names the embedded content for assistive technology; loading can be deferred; sandbox and allow limit capabilities; and responsive sizing must prevent the nested document from breaking the parent layout.
